notes: WIN-NAV-004. Behind the Material file-widget theme, NotebookBar::UpdateBackground() (vcl/source/control/notebookbar.cxx) resolves the ribbon group-area background wash to the @surface role (docs/design/05-navigation.md 4.1, 'Group area ... @surface fill') through vcl::MaterialTokens instead of the four legacy per-module accent tints (Writer #1a85d1, Calc #3cbc45, Impress #e75729, Draw #e5b443). Under the native/default theme every per-module accent Merge() call is retained byte-for-byte and no Material code path runs, so keyboard, mouse, a11y and RTL behaviour are untouched. SCOPE (deliberate honest narrowing): this pins the group-area background wash ONLY. It is NOT the 38px tab-row @surface-container band, NOT the tab-row @outline-variant bottom rule, and NOT the 96px group height / 8px padding / command-chip .ui geometry -- a single NotebookBar Wallpaper colour cannot carry the tab-row band and group area as two distinct tokens simultaneously, so those remain .ui/build-bound work outside this contract and this contract must never be cited as tab-row-band or .ui-geometry coverage. Replacing the recognisable per-module blue/green/orange/yellow tint under the Material theme is an intentional, design-directed change (05-navigation.md 4.1), not a regression. Source evidence only: no native build, notebookbar pixels, or runtime interaction are claimed (runtime_verified false).

group_area.note: docs/design/05-navigation.md 4.1: 'Group area | 96px tall, 8px padding | @surface fill'. Only the @surface fill token is pinned here; the 96px height, 8px padding, @outline-variant inter-group dividers and big/small/chip command sizing remain .ui/build-bound work outside this contract.
